notes: DMTF standard for desktop PC management; DMI 2.0 released June 1998; end-of-life declared March 31 2005; superseded by CIM and WMI
HP's flagship enterprise PC line; first DMI-compliant PCs (1992); discontinued 2002 after HP-Compaq merger; replaced by HP Evo
DMI-compliant desktop management suite; included AssetTOOLS, ConfigTOOLS, FlashTOOL, SafeTOOLS, ProtectTOOLS; discontinued with Vectra line
HP's network and systems management platform; rebranded as HP Software Division products in 2007; sold to MicroFocus; now part of OpenText
Remote PC power-on using Magic Packet technology co-developed with AMD; predecessor of Wake-on-LAN standard
Remote power-on technology; evolved into Wake-on-LAN (WOL) standard; still widely used in enterprise PC management
Compaq's proprietary desktop management system; required Compaq hardware and invited third-party partners; discontinued after HP acquisition of Compaq 2002
Hard drive health monitoring technology; still widely used in modern storage devices
DMTF standard that superseded DMI; foundation for WMI and modern IT management
Microsoft's implementation of CIM/WBEM that became the dominant desktop management standard; shipped with Windows 2000
